div.p: Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to his sheriff and bailies of Dumfries Greeting: Know that we have inspected the charters of the renowned kings David and Malcolm our ancestors, and of the lord King William our father, in which it is contained that the abbot and covnent of Holyrood ought so to have free court in the land of Dunrod – which they hold in Galloway of the gift of Fergus and his heirs – as in the other lands that they have of the gift of our ancestors, that is, in Broughton and Hamera [now Whitekirk]. Wherefore we command you and firmly enjoin, that you should by no means permit that any of the heirs of Galloway, or their bailies, obstruct the said abbot and convent, that they may rather have their free court in the said land of Dunrod respecting everything that ought justly to pertain to them; doing as much lest because of your failure it be necessary for the said abbot and convent to return to us once more to complain about this matter. As witnesses, Henry de Balliol, chamberlain, John de Vaux, Nicholas de Soulis, at Edinburgh on the 16th day of February, in the thirty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
